slides.title: Today
slides.markdown:
## Goals

- Review syllabus
- Review course goals
- Review brownfield development
- Review greenfield development
- Setup Canvas LMS

slides.title: The course rule
slides.markdown:
## Conductor, not every violin

- You’re **not** graded on hand-writing implementation line by line.
- You **are** graded on prompts, markdown, commands, and critique.
- Think: **score + rehearsal notes**, not playing every note yourself.

slides.title: Your toolbox
slides.markdown:
## Pick one lane and get fluent

- **Copilot + VS Code**, **Claude Code**, or **Cursor** — any is fine, but it must be the pro edition.
- Goal is **repeatable** sessions in a real editor or CLI agent.
- Brand matters less than **muscle memory** by week three.

slides.title: Brownfield vs greenfield
slides.markdown:
## Renovate vs new build

- **Brownfield:** working **inside** walls, wiring, and habits that already exist.
- **Greenfield:** empty lot — freedom, but you invent every convention.
- Industry spends most time in **brownfield**; this course leans that way too.

slides.title: Context window
slides.markdown:
## Desk space, not a warehouse

- The model only “sees” what fits on the **desk** at once (tokens).
- What’s **off the desk** doesn’t vote on the answer until you bring it back.
- Bigger desk helps; **curating** what sits on it still wins.

slides.title: Context management
slides.markdown:
## Leave the desk ~60% clear

- **~40% full** ≈ room to think; jam-packed ≈ noise drowns signal.
- Prefer **indexes and summaries** over pasting whole trees.
- Same idea whether you measure tokens or **estimate** — stay disciplined.

slides.title: Lab 1 (direction)
slides.markdown:
## Your copy, your cloud bench

- **Fork** = your own copy of the course repo on GitHub — steps live on the lab page.
- **Learner Lab + EC2** = a small **workbench in the cloud**, not your laptop as build farm.
- **SSH / Remote SSH** = **sit at that bench** from your machine.

slides.title: Lab 2 (teaser)
slides.markdown:
## Repo analyst agent — three pillars

- **Index** = **map** so you don’t re-walk the whole forest every time.
- **Context cap** = same “desk space” rule, but for repo dives.
- **Scripts** = **power tools**; the LLM holds the **blueprint**, not the saw.
